input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS → iOS + Android:

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS builds)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install Xcode (full, not just Command Line Tools)
- After install, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  ```
- Accept the license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- **Discover version:** MAUI docs list the minimum Xcode version per .NET release at [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/)

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** release that supports MAUI
- Use the macOS **Arm64** installer if on Apple Silicon, **x64** for Intel
- **Discover version:** The MAUI release notes page lists compatible .NET SDK versions. Currently .NET 8 (LTS) or .NET 9 are typical targets.
- Verify: `dotnet --version`

## 3. .NET MAUI Workload

- Installed via the SDK itself:
  ```
  dotnet workload install maui
  ```
- This pulls in the iOS, Android, and platform-specific workloads automatically.
- **Discover available versions:** `dotnet workload search maui`
- Verify: `dotnet workload list` (should show `maui` as installed)

## 4. Android SDK / JDK (Android builds)

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components automatically, but you need to verify:

- **JDK:** The MAUI workload bundles Microsoft's OpenJDK. No separate install needed.
- **Android SDK:** Managed via the workload. To confirm or adjust:
  ```
  dotnet android sdk list
  dotnet android sdk install --package "platforms;android-35"
  ```
- If you prefer a standalone manager, install **Android Studio** from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio), but this is **optional** for MAUI — the workload handles it.
- Verify: `dotnet android sdk list` shows installed platforms/build-tools.

## 5. (Optional) IDE

| IDE | Source | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------|
| **Visual Studio Code** | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) | + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extensions |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** | **Discontinued** — do not install | |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) | Full MAUI support |

VS Code with the **.NET MAUI Extension** (ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) is the current official Microsoft-recommended path on macOS.

## 6. Verify Everything End-to-End

```bash
# 1. Check SDK
dotnet --info

# 2. Check workloads
dotnet workload list

# 3. Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiVerify
cd MauiVerify

# 4. Build for both targets
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios        # or net8.0-ios
dotnet build -f net9.0-android    # or net8.0-android

# 5. Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run

# 6. Run on Android emulator (create one first via Android SDK)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

If all four builds succeed and the app launches on a simulator/emulator, your environment is fully working.

## Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

| Component | How to find the right version |
|-----------|-------------------------------|
| .NET SDK | [github.com/dotnet/maui/releases](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) — each release notes its minimum SDK |
| Xcode | [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/ios/](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/) — lists min Xcode per release |
| Android API | `dotnet workload install maui` auto-selects; override via project `<TargetFramework>` |
| MAUI workload | Always matches your SDK band — `dotnet workload update` keeps it current |
